    inf_le_inf_left _ hUr
  have e1 := res_comp (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M)
      (A := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) (B := U) (C := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ U)
      hUr inf_le_right (inf_le_right.trans hUr) br
  have e2 := res_comp (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M) (A := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)
      (B := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)
      (C := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ U) inf_le_right hCB (inf_le_right.trans hUr) br
  have e3 := res_comp (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M) (A := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
      (B := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)
      (C := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ U) inf_le_left hCB inf_le_left y
  have hms := LinearMap.map_smul ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map (homOfLE hCB).op).hom
      (f ^ N) (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
          ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom y)
  exact e1.trans (e2.symm.trans ((congrArg _ hbr).trans (hms.trans (congrArg (f ^ N • ·) e3))))

end Scheme.Modules

/-- **Surjectivity field of the section-localization descent.**  With the per-cover-element (and
per-overlap) localization data `Hfr`, every section `y` over `D(f)` becomes, after multiplying by a
power of `f`, the restriction of a global section.  The classical Hartshorne II.5.3 argument: on each
`D(r)` of a finite basic-open cover `{D(r)}` of `Spec R`, `y|_{D(f) ⊓ D(r)}` is `f^{-N}` times the
restriction of a section `b_r` on `D(r)` (`IsLocalizedModule.surj` of `Hfr` at `D(r)`, with a common
power `N`); on overlaps the `b_r` agree after a further power `f^P` (`descent_overlap_agree` +
`IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq` of `Hfr` at `D(r) ⊓ D(r')`), so `f^P • b_r` glue
(`TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing'`) to a global `x` with `x|_{D(f)} = f^{N+P} • y` (by sheaf
separatedness over the cover `{D(f) ⊓ D(r)}` of `D(f)`).  Project-local: `Hfr` is the gated P1
local-tilde data. -/
private lemma descent_surj {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules) (f : R)
    (t : Finset R) (hspan : Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤)
    (Hfr : ∀ U : (Spec R).Opens, (∃ r ∈ t, U ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) →
      IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ U ≤ U)).op).hom)
    (y : ToType ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) :
    ∃ (x : ToType ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤))) (n : ℕ),
      f ^ n • y = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x := by
  classical
  -- Stage 1: per cover element a section `a r` and a power `m r`.
  have perr : ∀ r : {x // x ∈ t}, ∃ (a : ToType ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj
        (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R))))) (m : ℕ),
        f ^ m • (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
            ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom y)
        = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
            ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R))).op).hom a := by
    rintro ⟨r, hr⟩
    have hloc := Hfr (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) ⟨r, hr, le_refl _⟩
    obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, hs⟩ := hloc.surj (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
            ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom y)
    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ := s.2
    refine ⟨a, m, ?_⟩
    have hsR : (s : R) • (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
            ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom y)
        = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
            ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)).op).hom a := hs
    rw [← hm] at hsR; exact hsR
  choose a m hm using perr
  -- Stage 2: common power N and normalized sections b r := f^(N - m r) • a r.
  set N := Finset.univ.sup m with hN
  have hb : ∀ r : {x // x ∈ t},
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
            ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R))).op).hom (f ^ (N - m r) • a r)
        = f ^ N • (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
            ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom y) := by
    intro r
    have hle : m r ≤ N := Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ r)
    set g := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
            ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R))).op).hom with hg
    have hms : g (f ^ (N - m r) • a r) = f ^ (N - m r) • g (a r) := LinearMap.map_smul g _ (a r)
    rw [hms, ← hm r, ← mul_smul, ← pow_add, Nat.sub_add_cancel hle]
  -- Stage 3: overlaps — common further power exists pairwise.
  have hover : ∀ i j : {x // x ∈ t}, ∃ p : ℕ,
      f ^ p • ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R) ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R)
          ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R))).op).hom (f ^ (N - m i) • a i)
      = f ^ p • ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R) ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R)
          ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R))).op).hom (f ^ (N - m j) • a j) := by
    intro i j
    have ai := descent_overlap_agree M f i N
      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R) ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R)) inf_le_left y _ (hb i)
    have aj := descent_overlap_agree M f j N
      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R) ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R)) inf_le_right y _ (hb j)
    have hloc := Hfr (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R) ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R))
      ⟨i, i.2, inf_le_left⟩
    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hloc.exists_of_eq (ai.trans aj.symm)
    obtain ⟨p, hp⟩ := c.2
    exact ⟨p, by rw [hp]; exact hc⟩
  choose p hp using hover
  -- Stage 4: global further power P, glue the compatible family.
  set P := Finset.univ.sup (fun i => Finset.univ.sup (fun j => p i j)) with hP
  have hPle : ∀ i j : {x // x ∈ t}, p i j ≤ P := fun i j =>
    le_trans (Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ j)) (Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ i))
  have hcompat : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf
      (fun r : {x // x ∈ t} => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R) : (Spec R).Opens))
      (fun r => f ^ P • (f ^ (N - m r) • a r)) := by
    intro i j
    show ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R) ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R)
          ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R))).op).hom (f ^ P • (f ^ (N - m i) • a i))
      = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R) ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R)
          ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R))).op).hom (f ^ P • (f ^ (N - m j) • a j))
    rw [LinearMap.map_smul, LinearMap.map_smul]
    have key : f ^ P • ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R) ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R)
          ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R))).op).hom (f ^ (N - m i) • a i)
      = f ^ P • ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (i:R) ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R)
          ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (j:R))).op).hom (f ^ (N - m j) • a j) := by
      have := hp i j
      rw [← Nat.sub_add_cancel (hPle i j), pow_add, mul_smul, mul_smul, this]
    exact key
  obtain ⟨x, hx, -⟩ := TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing' (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M)
    (fun r : {x // x ∈ t} => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R) : (Spec R).Opens)) ⊤
    (fun r => homOfLE le_top) (le_of_eq (iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top hspan).symm)
    (fun r => f ^ P • (f ^ (N - m r) • a r)) hcompat
  -- Stage 5: x|_{D(f)} = f^(N+P) • y, by separatedness over the cover {D(f) ⊓ D(r)} of D(f).
  refine ⟨x, N + P, ?_⟩
  have hcoverDf : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
      ≤ ⨆ r : {x // x ∈ t}, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)) := by
    rw [← inf_iSup_eq, iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top hspan, inf_top_eq]
  refine TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq' (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M)
    (fun r : {x // x ∈ t} => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)))
    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) (fun r => homOfLE inf_le_left) hcoverDf
    (f ^ (N + P) • y)
    (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
      (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x) ?_
  intro r
  -- LHS = f^(N+P) • (y|_{D(f) ⊓ D(r)})
  show ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
      (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
        ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom (f ^ (N + P) • y)
    = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
      (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
        ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom
      (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x)
  -- compute the right-hand side via x|_{D(r)} = f^P • b r
  have ex := res_comp (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M) (A := ⊤) (B := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
      (C := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R))
      le_top inf_le_left le_top x
  have ex2 := res_comp (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M) (A := ⊤) (B := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R))
      (C := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R))
      le_top inf_le_right le_top x
  have hxr := hx r

  have hRHS : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
      (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
        ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom
      (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x)
    = f ^ (N + P) • (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (inf_le_left : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R)
          ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom y) := by
    rw [ex, ← ex2, hxr]
    rw [LinearMap.map_smul, hb r, smul_smul, ← pow_add, Nat.add_comm P N]
  rw [hRHS, LinearMap.map_smul]

end Scheme.Modules

/-- **Section-localization descent from a local-tilde cover (gap1 keystone, D), cover form.**  Let
`M` be a sheaf of modules on `Spec R` and `f : R`.  Suppose `{D(r)}_{r ∈ t}` is a finite basic-open
cover of `Spec R` (`Ideal.span t = ⊤`) and for every open `U` contained in some cover member `D(r)`
(in particular each `D(r)` and each overlap `D(r) ⊓ D(r')`) the basic-open restriction
`Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, D(f) ⊓ U)` is a localization at `powers f` (`Hfr` — the gated P1 local-tilde data).
Then the section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.

This is the project-internal finite-equalizer/flatness descent of Stacks `lemma-invert-f-sections`
(Hartshorne II.5.3), built without the global affine `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` equivalence (which is
gap1 itself): `map_units` holds for arbitrary `M` (`map_units_restrict_basicOpen`), `surj'` is
`descent_surj`, and `exists_of_eq` is `descent_smul_eq_zero`.  The named gap1 keystone
`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` for quasi-coherent `M` is this lemma instantiated at the cover
`exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData` once `Hfr` is produced from
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` (the slice→Spec-`R_r` section transport, the remaining gated
step).  Project-local. -/
theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules)
    (f : R) (t : Finset R) (hspan : Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤)
    (Hfr : ∀ U : (Spec R).Opens, (∃ r ∈ t, U ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) →

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean — QUOT gap1-D (iter-035)

## Summary
- **Declarations added: 6** (all axiom-clean, `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`).
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover` (theorem,
    **non-private**, the keystone) — line ~1630.
  - `iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top` (private) — line ~1325.
  - `res_comp` (private) — line ~1338.
  - `descent_smul_eq_zero` (private, the `exists_of_eq` engine) — line ~1356.
  - `descent_overlap_agree` (private, overlap-agreement bookkeeping) — line ~1422.
  - `descent_surj` (private, the `surj` field) — line ~1462.
- **Declarations blocked: 0** sorries introduced. The *named* keystone
  `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` (quasi-coherent `M`) is NOT added — its only missing
  ingredient is the gated `Hfr` per-piece data (see "Why I stopped").
- **Sorry count: 4 → 4** (unchanged; the 4 are the pre-existing protected stubs at L126/165/201/228).
  No new sorries. The gap1-D content is all-new decls, not stub fills.

## isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover (RESOLVED — axiom-clean)
- **What it is:** the project-internal finite-cover sheaf-gluing descent of Stacks
  `lemma-invert-f-sections` / Hartshorne II.5.3, in **cover-hypothesis form**. For `M : (Spec R).Modules`,
  `f : R`, a finite basic-open cover `{D(r)}_{r∈t}` (`Ideal.span t = ⊤`), and the per-piece localization
  data
  ```
  Hfr : ∀ U, (∃ r ∈ t, U ≤ D(r)) → IsLocalizedModule (powers f) (Γ(M,U) → Γ(M, D(f)⊓U))
  ```
  it proves `IsLocalizedModule (powers f) (Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f)))`.
- **Approach:** assembled the 3 `IsLocalizedModule` fields:
  - `map_units` := the already-present `map_units_restrict_basicOpen M f` (holds for arbitrary `M`).
  - `surj` := `descent_surj` (per-`D(r)` surjectivity with a common power `N`, overlap agreement up to a
    further power `P` via `descent_overlap_agree` + `IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq`, glue the
    `f^P`-scaled family by `TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing'`, conclude on `D(f)` by separatedness
    `TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq'`).
  - `exists_of_eq` := `descent_smul_eq_zero` (a global section vanishing on `D(f)` is killed by a power
    of `f`: per cover element `exists_of_eq` of `Hfr` + finite `Finset.sup` + separatedness).
- **Key Mathlib infrastructure relied on:** `TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing'`,
  `TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq'`, `TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible` (unfolds via `rfl` to
  `Opens.infLELeft/infLERight`), `IsLocalizedModule.surj` / `.exists_of_eq`,
  `PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff'`, `inf_iSup_eq` (Opens is a frame),
  `LinearMap.map_smul`. Crucially the proof **does NOT route through the global affine equivalence**
  (`QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R`, which is gap1 itself).
- **Result:** RESOLVED, `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. Build of
  `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme` succeeds (8317 jobs, warnings only).

## isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent (NOT ADDED — gated on `Hfr`)
- **What remains:** the *named* keystone for quasi-coherent `M` is exactly
  ```
  isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover M f t hspan Hfr
  ```
  with `t, hspan` from `exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData M q` (q from
  `‹M.IsQuasicoherent›.nonempty_quasicoherentData.some`). The ONLY missing piece is producing `Hfr`:
  for `U ≤ D(r) ≤ q.X i`, `Γ(M,U) → Γ(M, D(f)⊓U)` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
- **Why gated:** `isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` (P1, done) gives `IsIso (fromTildeΓ)` for the
  **transported** model `M|_{D(r)}` realised over `Spec R_r` (`pullback isoSpec.inv ∘ pullback ι ∘
  pullback ι`); `isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ` then localizes ITS sections
  `Γ(M'_transported, ⊤) → Γ(M'_transported, D(f'))`. The remaining step is the **slice→Spec-`R_r`
  section transport** that identifies `Γ(M'_transported, ⊤) ≅ Γ(M, D(r))` and
  `Γ(M'_transported, D(f')) ≅ Γ(M, D(f)⊓D(r))` *intertwining the restriction maps* — the
  `Scheme.Modules.pullback`-along-open-immersion vs. presheaf-restriction comparison, which has no
  Mathlib support at the pinned commit (already flagged at the `exists_finite_basicOpen_...` docstring).
- **Next step (precise):** build
  `Γ((pullback (Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M, ⊤) ≅ Γ(M, q.X i)` (pullback-along-open-immersion global
  sections = sections over the image) and its naturality in the open, then chain through the two further
  pullbacks of P1 to land `Hfr`. Then `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` and gap1
  (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent = isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict M
  (isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent M ·)`) are both one-liners.

## Dead-end warnings (do NOT retry)
- `bijective_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory.LocalProperties.Exactness`) does NOT give a
  shortcut: reducing `IsLocalizedModule θ` to bijectivity of the lift and localizing at the cover
  symmetrically demands `Away r`-localizations of the **target** `Γ(M,D(f))` at each `r`, which are not
  available (`M|_{D(f)}` is not known to be a tilde). The manual `surj`/`exists_of_eq` via the sheaf
  condition is the honest route (it only needs the `Γ(M,D(r))` localizations, available via `Hfr`).
- `rw [LinearMap.map_smul]` / `rw [res_comp …]` / `rw [ms_i]` repeatedly fail to match the
  `ModuleCat.Hom.hom (… .presheaf.map (homOfLE ⋯).op)` coercion even on syntactically-identical terms
  (the elided `homOfLE` proofs defeat `rw`). Throughout, **term-mode** (`.trans`, `congrArg`, fully-
  applied `LinearMap.map_smul g a x`, `res_comp F …` with explicit `A B C`) is the reliable pattern;
  `set g := …` before `rw [hms]` also works. Avoid `set` on whole-sheaf expressions — it reverts
  bound vars / refolds inside hypotheses (broke `e1`/hbr matching twice).

## Needs blueprint entry
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover`** (non-private, new) —
  will be flagged by `archon dag-query unmatched`. The existing blueprint block
  `lem:section_localization_descent` (Picard_QuotScheme.tex, L3533) pins
  `\lean{…isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent}` (the named, still-gated form). The planner should either
  (a) repoint that `\lean{}` to `…isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover` and add a thin downstream
  block for the named form, or (b) add a dedicated block for the cover form `\uses`-wired to
  `lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1),
  `lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData`, `lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib`,
  `lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib`. Its proof relies on: `map_units_restrict_basicOpen`,
  `descent_surj` (private), `descent_smul_eq_zero` (private), `descent_overlap_agree` (private),
  `res_comp` (private), `iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top` (private).
- The 5 private helpers owe no blueprint block (private, per the standing-notes convention for
  `rotMid`/`transitionInvImageMatrix`/etc.).

## Why I stopped
**Real progress: 6 axiom-clean declarations added**, capturing the *entire* sheaf-theoretic content of
the gap1-D descent:
- `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover` (keystone, non-private, ~L1630) — the full Hartshorne
  II.5.3 descent in cover form, all three `IsLocalizedModule` fields assembled, axiom-clean.
- `descent_surj` (~L1462), `descent_smul_eq_zero` (~L1356), `descent_overlap_agree` (~L1422),
  `res_comp` (~L1338), `iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top` (~L1325) — the load-bearing private lemmas.

The named keystone `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` (quasi-coherent `M`) is **not** added because
its sole remaining input `Hfr` is the slice→`Spec R_r` section-transport, which is the documented
Mathlib-absent step (confirmed by prior iters; same wall as P1's object-level transport). This is a
genuine mathematical/infrastructure blocker, not a route choice — I verified the only in-file path to
`Hfr` (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` → `isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`) bottoms
out at the un-built `Γ(pullback ι M, ⊤) ≅ Γ(M, image)` comparison. The precise next ingredient is named
above. Everything I built is reusable verbatim once `Hfr` lands: gap1 then closes in two one-liners.

---
name: quot-gap1d-descent-and-modulecat-restriction-termmode
description: gap1-D section-localization descent landed axiom-clean in cover form; ModuleCat sheaf-restriction proofs need term-mode (rw fails on elided homOfLE proofs).
metadata:
  type: project
---

QUOT gap1-D (iter-035, `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean`): the section-localization
descent (Stacks `lemma-invert-f-sections` / Hartshorne II.5.3) is **DONE axiom-clean in cover form**
as `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover` (non-private,
keystone). It takes a finite basic-open cover `{D(r)}_{r∈t}` (`span t = ⊤`) and per-piece data
`Hfr : ∀ U, (∃ r∈t, U ≤ D(r)) → IsLocalizedModule (powers f) (Γ(M,U)→Γ(M,D(f)⊓U))`, and proves
`IsLocalizedModule (powers f) (Γ(M,⊤)→Γ(M,D(f)))`. Three fields: `map_units` =
`map_units_restrict_basicOpen` (arbitrary M); `surj` = private `descent_surj` (common power N + overlap
agreement up to power P via `descent_overlap_agree` + `existsUnique_gluing'` + `eq_of_locally_eq'`);
`exists_of_eq` = private `descent_smul_eq_zero`. Does NOT use the global `QCoh≃Mod` equivalence (= gap1
itself). Private helpers: `res_comp`, `iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top`, `descent_smul_eq_zero`,
`descent_overlap_agree`, `descent_surj`.

**Remaining gate** for the *named* `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` (quasi-coherent M) and hence
gap1: producing `Hfr`. P1 (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`) gives `IsIso fromTildeΓ` for the
**transported** `M|_{D(r)}` over `Spec R_r`; `isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ` localizes
its sections — but identifying `Γ(M'_transported,⊤) ≅ Γ(M,D(r))` and `Γ(…,D(f')) ≅ Γ(M,D(f)⊓D(r))`
intertwining restriction maps (the `Scheme.Modules.pullback`-along-open-immersion vs presheaf-
restriction comparison) is Mathlib-absent. Next ingredient: `Γ((pullback (Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M,⊤) ≅
Γ(M, q.X i)` natural in the open. Once `Hfr` lands, gap1 closes in two one-liners via
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict`. See [[sheafofmodules-slice-presentation-transport-tricks]].

**Reusable technique (QuotScheme + FBC):** for sheaf-of-modules restriction maps
`((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom`, `rw` REPEATEDLY fails to match
syntactically-identical subterms because the elided `homOfLE ⋯` proofs and `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` /
`ConcreteCategory.hom` coercions defeat the matcher. Use **term-mode** instead:
- composition: `res_comp F (A:=…)(B:=…)(C:=…) hBA hCB hCA y` (proved by
  `rw [← ModuleCat.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]; rfl`), chained with `.trans`/`.symm`.
- `map_smul`: fully-applied term `LinearMap.map_smul g a x`, not `rw [map_smul]`.
- bridge `ConcreteCategory.hom`/`.obj` (from `eq_of_locally_eq'`/`existsUnique_gluing'`) to
  `ModuleCat.Hom.hom`/`.presheaf` with `change` (defeq), then close term-mode.
- AVOID `set` on whole-sheaf expressions: it reverts bound vars / refolds inside hypotheses, breaking
  later matching. `set g := (…).hom` on a single map is fine.
- `IsLocalizedModule` fields are `map_units`, `surj`, `exists_of_eq` (NOT `surj'`). Submonoid-elt smul
  `c • x` is defeq `(↑c) • x` (so `exact` closes across them). `IsCompatible` unfolds by `rfl` to
  `Opens.infLELeft/infLERight`.

I've completed a substantial axiom-clean delivery for the QUOT gap1-D lane. Here's the summary.

## What I built

The **gap1-D section-localization descent** (Stacks `lemma-invert-f-sections` / Hartshorne II.5.3) is now landed **axiom-clean in cover form** — 6 new declarations, build passes (8317 jobs), `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`:

- **`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover`** (the keystone, non-private) — given a finite basic-open cover `{D(r)}` of `Spec R` and the per-piece localization data `Hfr`, proves `Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`. All three `IsLocalizedModule` fields assembled, **without** routing through the global `QCoh ≃ Mod` equivalence (which is gap1 itself).
- Five private engines: `descent_surj` (surjectivity via common-power normalization + overlap agreement + sheaf gluing), `descent_smul_eq_zero` (the `exists_of_eq`/separatedness engine), `descent_overlap_agree`, `res_comp`, `iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top`.

The genuinely hard, novel content — the overlap-agreement and the torsion/separatedness arguments over the cover — is all proved.

## Why the *named* keystone isn't added

`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` (for quasi-coherent `M`) = the cover form instantiated, with the **only** missing input being `Hfr`. I verified that producing `Hfr` requires the **slice→`Spec R_r` section transport** (identifying `Γ(M'_transported,⊤) ≅ Γ(M,D(r))` intertwining restriction maps) — the documented Mathlib-absent step, the same wall P1 crossed at the object level. This is a genuine infrastructure blocker, not a route choice; the precise next ingredient (`Γ((pullback ι).obj M, ⊤) ≅ Γ(M, image)`) is named in the handoff. Once `Hfr` lands, gap1 closes in two one-liners.

I also recorded the reusable term-mode technique (term-mode `.trans`/`congrArg`/applied `map_smul`/`res_comp` beats `rw` for ModuleCat sheaf restriction maps, since elided `homOfLE` proofs defeat `rw`) to memory, and flagged the one new non-private decl for blueprint coverage in `task_results/AlgebraicJacobian_Picard_QuotScheme.md`.
